review_title: Automating manual, repetitive tasks have never been easier!
description: Active Batch handles all of our nightly batch jobs including but not limited to file transfers between SFTP sites, SSRS report generation, file encryption/decryption and much more. We have several use cases for using Active Batch but one of the main drivers was having a solution to house all of our Windows Task Scheduler jobs, while task scheduler is great, it was a pain to manage and support. With Active Batch, we now have a single solution where we can see which jobs are running, how long they are running for, if they failed, and when they would run next, etc. We currently have about 1000 jobs in different Active Batch environments.
author: Khoa N.
pros: ActiveBatch treats all items as objects; this allows for object reuse when building jobs and also reduces the overhead of maintaining and supporting multiple objects.
cons: Having to upgrade each of the execution agents, there needs to be a process where the agents are automatically updated when there is a new version available.

review_title: All the tools you need under one roof
description: Biggest problems solved are scheduling and job dependency issues. ActiveBatch allows you to make sure jobs run in the order you need them to. Building a workflow on job step dependency where on job kicks off the next as expected. Or utilizing constraints to not run a job multiple times a day if it has already run. It also has a great feedback alert system. to send emails, text messages, etc to let you know when a job has succeeded or failed. New apps allow you to manage jobs from your phone.
author: Anthony R.
pros: It has tons of features, and options to integrate it's automation into many third party platforms for backups, email, sharepoint, tracking systems. The options for coding automation in your comfort zone is also great. Whether you script in ksh, bsh, cmd, bat, powershell, js, or vb; you can take existing workflows and start scheduling them into ActiveBatch seamlessly. Or do as I do; and integrate all those skills into a jobs library and create a custom ActiveBatch workflow.
cons: Some of those third party integration point cost extra. Probably expected. There is some challenges with security tokens when migrating to new systems.

They are always ready to assist with any questions or issues. The platform has improved the productivity and overall efficiency.

review_title: Top Notch Automation Capability
description: Overall, ActiveBatch is a very solid and capable package that gives you almost everything you could ever want in automation.
author: Tyson H.
pros: ActiveBatch is the backbone of our automation operations. We use it to automate a wide variety of tasks from running our own custom data processing software to retrieving FTP to monitoring server status. * The scheduler has amazing variety and flexibility for automating tasks: jobs within plans, variables that change parameters dynamically, constraints on other tasks, triggers for other tasks. * Monitoring status of jobs and plans can be done in a variety of ways, through a UI, through email or other communication, even automated reporting. Almost however you want to look at status, ActiveBatch has you covered. * Great support for integration with other technologies and services. They have a number of packages for integration with third party systems. You can fairly easily integrate with command line tools, custom libraries or web APIs to create custom links. We've even used Powershell to automate ActiveBatch itself. * Their tech support has always been quick to respond and genuinely willing to dig deep to help solve the problem.
cons: Only a couple cons come to mind. * With increased flexibility and capability come increased complexity. It's easy to get a couple jobs going, but there is a learning curve to use the full capability. There is no free training (like self-guided course), though perhaps that's changed since the last time I looked, and the documentation is difficult to wade through. * We do see quirky behavior indicative of small bugs that will manifest themselves after weeks of operation. Although I would consider it stable, every now and again something strange happens (weeks between occurrences) and it's time to restart the service. It's not the same thing every time and support has always helped us navigate unexpected behavior.

review_title: Active Batch is inclining!!
description: Its good, Cost effective, Its Pro active Support
author: girish K.
pros: Active Batch highly secured and end to end Encrypted, It Prevents un authorised access, It can perform quick actions on Problem workflows, It Is a Job Scheduler and Batch Scheduling too.
cons: It Should be carry through third party vendors, More Social Marketing is Required to Explore and enhance the Application

review_title: My experiences over the years in ActiveBatch
description: We have used ActiveBatch for the last 5 years and are we happy. So happy that we have moved all our Automation and scheduling to ActiveBatch and that shows the trust few have in the product
author: Lourens P.
pros: Previously we have used a few scheduling systems but in recent years we have moved all our automation and scheduling to ActiveBatch. Which shows the confidence we have in the product The central location of all our workflows and an operation to view running, failed and successful jobs make ActiveBatch an outstanding product. The execute agent and job scheduler is very stable. ActiveBatch scheduler is brilliant and the use of custom dates and calendars makes scheduling powerful. The PowerShell script option in AB makes scheduling of PowerShell scripts easy. Reuse and reference of scripts in workflows make development quicker. The use of variables on Jobs, schedulers, folders, triggers and task gives us a lot of variety and flexibility. The support team is very good and always eager to help. The only issues we have is the difference in time zones in response from the support team due to that. But it has imporved over the last few months.
cons: What I found over the years is when a new version of AB has been released, jobs that were working in the previous release not working in the new version. The new GUI of v12 looks good but is a lot slower and crashes more often than the previous version. To do a simple task and using the internal steps like reading a from DB, loop through the records, setting values to a condition can be a confusing thing. Lately, we have been switching all our jobs that were using job steps to PowerShell scripts because we have more control and options in Powershell.

review_title: The right software solution for your business
author: Haripriya V.
pros: Its an open-source platform to review about choosing a software and we have access to read the reviews of all customers just to plan before getting into it.It helps a lot in my day to day activities like automation of my workloads.Its user-friendly (easy-to-use) UI so any non IT person can easily get hands-on on this software.Its compatible with different organizations and Active Batch can easily integrate with other applications which makes it easy to access and automates all workloads in different platforms.Customer support team is excellent which makes us resolve our queries in short span of time
cons: Cost is something which is a con if its for small organizations.Needs bit more detailed documentation and improvement in the UI part to make it more user-friendly.Comparing to other software, the reporting capabilities are bit less which should be improved better with IT experts.

review_title: Very robust platform for workflow managed and scheduled jobs
description: Very useful but sometimes-tough-to-navigate tool. Most users will not ever utilize it to its full potential since there are so many options and areas of configuration. There is a web version and an installed application version, which have most if not all of the same features.
author: Philip C.
pros: The ability to manage workflows is intuitive once you know how things work. Being able to hit external endpoints or platforms (e.g. web API, Azure cloud functions, etc) is very handy and being able to control the timing is important for doing things during off-hours.
cons: The learning curve is fairly intense, as there are many types of objects that do different things. I haven't found any great video tutorial series that cover the basics, but maybe there are some trainings available directly from AB. For our team it was imperative that we had some knowledge transfer from more senior members who know their way around the platform.
